orb_30m.name: ORB 30m
orb_30m.description: Opening Range Breakout on the first 30 minutes of RTH session

params.Entry: First 5m close beyond the range (max 1 trade/day)
params.Stop: Far side of opening range (price-based, not time-based)

ema_cross_5m.name: EMA Cross 5m
ema_cross_5m.description: EMA 9/100 crossover on 5-minute RTH bars with trend and volatility filters. Bilateral (deployed 2026-07-01) — long and short EMA9x100 crosses; short side passed the gate suite vs the deployed trend20 ORB.

params.Trend Filter: EMA 200 (price above EMA200 = long, below = short)
params.Direction: Bilateral — long AND short (short side deployed 2026-07-01; passed the gate suite vs the DEPLOYED trend20 ORB)
params.Entry: Long or short cross, next-bar open (market order)
params.Exit: Reverse cross flips direction, or EOD flat (whichever first)
params.2026 OOS PF: 0.98 (bilateral — short side cold in 2026, held per governance)

dc_exit3.name: DC Mean Reversion
dc_exit3.description: Donchian Channel daily mean reversion — buy when RTH close < prior 10-day low; exit on first up-close or 5-day time stop. Long only.

params.Signal: Daily close < prior 10-day low (DC lower band, shifted)
params.Exit (up-close): First day where close > signal-day close

recent.reason: Strategy requires tick-level order-flow data (buy/sell tick counts per 5m bar) aggregated from real-time tick feeds, which exceeds stateless template architecture. Entry logic depends on tick imbalance ratio (buy_ticks / total_ticks > 0.58) as a real-time signal; exit depends on RSI(14) mean-reversion state machine (monitoring continuous RSI trajectory to 50±3 band). This is a path-dependent, stateful strategy requiring live tick-disaggregation and multi-indicator state tracking across position lifecycle. The edge hypothesis (order-flow decoupling from momentum in high-velocity regimes) is coherent and potentially valid, but the mechanics cannot be expressed as a deterministic, stateless entry/exit rule set in any of the provided templates.

Adaptive trailing stop (swing low update logic) violates deterministic backtest architecture; stop price is path-dependent and requires real-time swing detection across 240-bar lookback, not computable from fixed OHLCV bar data alone. VIX real-time dependency also incompatible with standard bar-replay engine.
Strategy shape requires manual backtest implementation

OVERRIDE of the 2026-07-04 01:04 PASS — engine artifact, not an edge: all 1,005 closed trades exited at 'target' (win 100%, PF inf, maxDD $0); the stop branch never fired for this param set and trades whose target never hit stayed OPEN and were excluded = closed-trade survivorship. Degeneracy guard added to auto_backtest (_is_degenerate) so impossible perfection auto-FAILs before any gate.
Era gate PASS | WF 0/16 sit-outs | Calmar inf | PF inf Sharpe 17.62

Strategy requires weekly COT data (external macro dataset with Friday release lag, Tuesday position-date mismatch) merged with 1m price bars—introduces severe look-ahead bias risk and data-alignment complexity incompatible with clean backtest architecture. COT is not a price-derived indicator; it violates the principle of using only OHLCV bars and their derivatives. Adaptive regime filtering on rolling 50th percentile of COT further complicates parameter stability across test periods.
WF 7/16 sit-outs; Calmar 0.05 < 0.5 (maxDD $-5,802 too deep vs return — undeployable)
Era gate FAIL (2010-2020 PF 0.86 | 2021-2026 PF 0.92); WF 11/16 sit-outs; Calmar -0.03 < 0.5 (maxDD $-15,763 too deep vs return — undeployable)
WF 7/16 sit-outs; Calmar 0.05 < 0.5 (maxDD $-5,802 too deep vs return — undeployable)
Era gate FAIL (2010-2020 PF 0.86 | 2021-2026 PF 0.92); WF 11/16 sit-outs; Calmar -0.03 < 0.5 (maxDD $-15,763 too deep vs return — undeployable)

research_discipline.headline: Alpha frontier explored & exhausted — ORB + EMA + DC on MNQ is the edge set.
research_discipline.summary: The territory is mapped — and as of 2026-07-03 the frontier is FULLY explored: every lever in the frontier memo (US-Treasury carry, multi-pair RV basket, ag seasonality, the ML/method frontier, global gov-bond carry, and the alt-data free tier) has been run to a pre-committed verdict, almost all for $0-to-pennies. ORB…

spend_ledger.note: Only ~$24 (NQ 1-min bars) backs the live book; everything else funded research that was rejected — the cost of knowing. A negative is a deliverable. ($264 paid + $1,125 due 2026-08-05; tick split est., final on the Aug-5 invoice.)

gates.name: Bilateral
gates.rule: tested long / short / bilateral — numbers decide direction.

gates.name: Walkforward
gates.rule: rolling 3y train → 1y OOS; 0 sit-out years (every OOS year PF > 1.0).

gates.name: Correlation
gates.rule: |corr| < 0.30 vs each deployed strategy's daily P&L (redundant = rejected).

gates.name: Calmar
gates.rule: annual return / maxDD ≥ 0.50 (drawdown recoverable within ~2 years).

gates.name: Fill-robustness
gates.rule: survives 2× cost AND (reversion) next-bar-open exits — catches edges that live only in optimistic precise fills (the "W71 trap").

failure_patterns.name: Edgeless after cost
failure_patterns.desc: Marginal PF (0.8–1.2) on lumpy drawdowns → fails Calmar/WF. Most classic indicators, volume profile, market structure, FX.

failure_patterns.name: Redundant with the deployed book
failure_patterns.desc: A re-expression of a factor ORB/EMA/DC already harvest → fails the corr<0.30 gate. EMA-short (corr ORB 0.375), compression (0.78)…

failure_patterns.name: In-sample mirage the walkforward kills
failure_patterns.desc: Great full-sample PF carried by a few years; loses in scattered OOS years → sit-outs. Supply/demand on NQ/silver, dry-up volume.

failure_patterns.name: Era-asymmetric
failure_patterns.desc: Works post-2021 only (era1 PF<1.0) → fails both-era. ORB-bilateral, thrust, FX configs.

failure_patterns.name: Fill-fragile (the W71 trap)
failure_patterns.desc: Edge lives in optimistic precise fills; collapses under next-bar-open exits / 2× cost. Supply/demand on gold/crude/copper/ZN, TPO va-rotation, the…

failure_patterns.name: Uncorrelated but edgeless instrument
failure_patterns.desc: Clean diversifier, no edge to monetize → bleeds. The entire instrument frontier (commodities, FX, rates, crypto).

failure_patterns.name: Look-ahead artifact
failure_patterns.desc: A bug, not an edge. TPO va-rotation (PF 4.46→0.98 once fixed), the reversion exit look-ahead. Caught by re-running with conservative fills.

investor_verdict.verdict:
No allocation today. You have ten live trades showing a $442 loss with massive average slippage (–10.9 pts), which either signals execution infrastructure problems or strategy fragility under real conditions that your backtest didn't capture. A 16-year backtest on a single micro-contract with $3.8k annual returns and Sharpe 1.36 is respectable work, but it's a theoretical proof-of-concept, not a fund—and the live-to-backtest gap is already red-flagging reality.

The core blocker: single-market concentration on MNQ with zero correlation diversification, combined with a live record too small and underwater to differentiate luck from edge. One bad news cycle in Nasdaq, and your three strategies all break in the same direction.

Next step to earn my check: Run live for 100+ trades minimum (6–9 months), hit positive cumulative P&L, and *simultaneously* demonstrate genuine uncorrelated edge in at least one other liquid micro product (ES, NQ, YM, or rate futures)—even if it's one of your three strats ported over. Show me slippage converges to backtest norms and you can scale to $50k AUM without blowup. Then we talk.

projections.note: 16-year backtest projection, 1-1-1 contracts (orb-ema-dc). ALL legs gated by the live trailing-252-trade-day PF rule (pf252>1.0, same as score_engine) and annualized over each leg's full calendar span. Book is ORB+EMA+DC (MNQ); Gold/Crude were retracted + removed 2026-06-28 (look-ahead artifact). EMA is BILATERAL (short side deployed 2026-07-01; passed gates vs deployed ORB).

risk.basis: raw ungated 16y backtest daily P&L, MNQ $2/pt (matches /corr)

sizing.basis: 16y deployed legs (raw, ungated worst-case); per-contract linear

kill_switch.note: Modeled in the book stats. Two-tier daily limit scales with book size: block new entries at −500 pts ($-1,000), flatten all at −750 pts ($-1,500). In-sample the hard tier is dormant (effect ≈ $0) — it's a catastrophe backstop, not a return lever.

ytd_equity.note: 2026 YTD running equity, 1-1-1 contracts (orb-ema-dc), deployed config (trend20 ORB, gated bilateral EMA, DC). Backtest/OOS model — not realized fills.
